subject: Sociology
title: Social Capital
description: A quick win: understand Social Capital.

hook.text: Two equally talented graduates apply for a job. One has an uncle in the company. Guess who gets hired.

buildup.text: Social capital is the value embedded in relationships: who you know, who trusts you, who will vouch for you.

discovery.text: Bourdieu argued that social capital reproduces class. Elite networks pass chances to insiders and shut others out.

twist.text: Social capital is invisible wealth. It doesn't show up on tax returns but shapes outcomes just as powerfully.

climax.text: Building social capital requires access: the right schools, clubs, and events. Access itself is unevenly distributed.

punchline.text: It's not just what you know. It's who knows you back.

quiz.question: What is social capital?
options: The value embedded in social relationships and networks
Money saved in a bank account
The total population of a society
